name: planning
description: Thirteen-skill pipeline that turns a vague idea into executed work, keeps each project's contracts honest, and gives a cross-project portfolio view. brainstorming validates the design via question-driven exploration — then routes designs with a structural surface to architecting-projects, which derives 2–4 stack-concrete architecture candidates, fans out one sonnet-pinned architecture-researcher agent per candidate in parallel (uncited claims discarded), presents a comparison matrix for explicit user approval — in re-architecture mode also inventorying the current layout so candidates carry a current→target migration map (ARCH-NN Migration map section) whose ordered steps seed the plan's stage order — and writes a parser-safe *-architecture.md with stable ARCH-NN section IDs beside the plan (zero portfolio-unify candidates by construction, locked by the validate-plan-parser fixture suite) that planning-projects consumes: structure-creating tasks cite their ARCH-ID and the final stage gate carries an architecture-conformance check; planning-projects produces a staged plan with phase gates and Red-Green cycles (reconciling backlog and workflow specs at research time) — and now decomposes big projects (>~6 stages / >~25 tasks, or multiple independently shippable workstreams) into 2–7 independently executable sub-plans linked by one master plan whose register carries cross-plan dependencies and per-entry integration gates (canonical format in references/master-plan-format.md, parser-safe by construction so a master yields zero portfolio-unify backlog candidates, locked by the validate-plan-parser CI fixture suite); executing-plans drives the plan with stage-gate checks — and executes master plans by running sub-plans in register dependency order (one per fresh session recommended; the master file is the cross-session handoff artifact), flipping register Status on each sub-plan close-out, running that entry's cross-plan gate checks, committing Sub-plan N green, and deferring version bumps to the single master close-out — a default independent-evaluator pass at gates and close-out, stage-boundary handoff notes for context resets, opens/closes backlog entries, and audits workflow specs at close-out — now with a git-bootstrap preflight (inits a repo when missing), run-to-completion execution that doesn't pause between green stages, a platform stage-verify hook at each gate (e.g. android-dev's android-stage-verify), a two-tier white-box code review wired into execution (per-task quick review where a Critical blocks within the cycle budget, plus a per-stage deep review at the gate) via git-github's read-only code-reviewer agent, and SemVer version bumps across every mirror at close-out; applying-design-handoff redesigns an app to precisely reproduce a Claude Design handoff pack (tokens, components, layout, assets), auto-detecting a local exported bundle or a live claude.ai design-system project via the DesignSync tool, inventorying the app and its workflow specs, and reshaping functionality to fit the design — the design wins, but every behavior change is gated through workflow-spec (Changes/Removes WF-*) with user sign-off — then delegating cross-platform code to the ui-* agents and the sonnet-pinned design-handoff-reproducer subagent (per-slice precise reproduction, rendering each slice with Playwright and following the mock as it builds on browser stacks) before a separate-evaluator fidelity-rubric verify loop that grades deterministic Playwright renders per breakpoint (against the pack's reference render where it carries one); executing-plans drives it for a design-handoff/redesign task and fires that loop as a stage-gate hook; dispatching-parallel-agents fans out independent tasks marked Parallel YES and routes each to a stack-matched subagent (rust-expert, ui-android, testing-expert, …) via a shared stack-routing reference that executing-plans also uses to delegate independent, output-heavy sequential tasks off the orchestrator's context — guarded by a CI-enforced validate-stack-routing.py check that fails when the routing table names an agent/skill the marketplace no longer ships; no-fafo-debugging fires at the start of any debugging/diagnostic work to enforce evidence-first root-cause analysis, and gathers that evidence autonomously (reads logs, runs read-only diagnostics, reproduces failures, builds interceptors/probes itself) so the user is pulled in only when genuinely blocked; backlog owns the deferred-work register at docs/backlog.md (now with `unify` and `complete` subcommands); workflow-spec owns the behavior contracts at docs/workflows/ used to detect regressions after change; project-maturity scaffolds and audits a per-project docs/MATURITY.md across six publishing-readiness axes (Documentation, Security, Packaging, UI/UX, i18n, Testing & CI); compass is the portfolio work orchestrator — a read-only now/next/review lane whose deterministic compass-scan.py (CI-guarded, reusing portfolio-unify's plan-parser regexes) rebuilds the cross-project work picture fresh each run (in-flight plan stages, backlog open/parked counts, maturity gaps, integration edges, git recency) and whose judgment layer ranks what to work on next (momentum > almost-shippable > unblocking > staleness), respects backlog Parked: annotations, cites evidence on every recommendation, and never writes portfolio artifacts; portfolio is a cross-project orchestrator that scans ~/dev/, unifies plans+backlogs per project, rolls up a global backlog index and a global maturity dashboard at ~/.claude/.; honest-gates defines the integrity contract for every verification gate — a gate is GREEN only when its real command ran here and passed, otherwise RED or BLOCKED (never a stubbed task, fabricated evidence, hidden exclusion, or self-graded miss), with escalation rules for blocked gates. Skills hand off to each other and were designed as a unit.
